name: International Studies

sections.label: World Language Requirement (two courses)
sections.description: Two world language courses numbered 125+ in Spanish or French, or 115+ in another language.

sections.label: Term-Long Study Abroad Experience
sections.description: Approved term-long study abroad experience, subject to IS program committee approval.

sections.label: Track: The Global South, International Relations, or Contemporary European Studies

options.title: The Global South — ECO-215 + ECO-436 (or approved alt) + POL-386 (or approved alt) + 2 regional electives
International Relations — POL-108 + POL-310 + 3 regional/IR electives
Contemporary European Studies — HIS-365 + POL-298 + 3 European culture courses

sections.note: Each track adds 4-5 additional required courses. Elective substitutions require IS coordinator approval. See full checklist for course lists.

notes: Three tracks available: The Global South, International Relations, and Contemporary European Studies. Each track adds approximately 5 courses beyond the core. Students considering economics-focused graduate work should consider the International Economics major instead.
